title: Bittersilkyromantic Casabe Stir-fry
description: A sophisticated twist on a traditional Mexican dish featuring casabe bread, perfectly combining bitterness and silken smoothness for an unforgettable culinary experience.
instructions: If using cure meat, slice it thinly.
Slice the casabe bread into thin slices and lightly toast them on a pan without any oil for about 2 minutes each side. Let cool slightly.
Finely chop garlic and red onion.
In a wok or large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add sliced casabe bread to the skillet and cook until golden brown on both sides (about 5 minutes total).
Add chopped garlic and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
If using cure meat, add it to the skillet now and stir-fry with garlic for another minute. If not using, skip this step.
Stir in finely chopped red onion and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until softened slightly.
Season with salt and pepper as desired.
Serve hot alongside a side of your choice or as a standalone dish.
